steps.title: Find the original median
steps.content: Data set A has $21$ values, so its median is the $11$th value in order. From the frequency table, the values in positions $9$ through $13$ are all $3$, so the median of data set A is $3$.

steps.title: Find the new median
steps.content:
Removing the erroneous value $19$ leaves $20$ values. The median is the average of the $10$th and $11$th values, and both are still $3$.
$$\frac{3+3}{2}=3$$

steps.title: Compare the medians
steps.content: Both medians are $3$, so the median of data set B is equal to the median of data set A. The answer is B.
